mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation confirms capacity utilization without price pressures, leading to positive equity returns as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth combined with low CPIAUCSL yields negative 20-day returns in over half of the triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the federal funds rate above 5.0 percent triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases when the federal funds rate breaches the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and generates positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary phase signals collapsing output and margin compression, forcing immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during recessions produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of observed instances.
